_meta.keyKnowledge: We write visual programs that use control structures, variables and user input.

_source.w: We write visual programs that use control structures, variables and user input.
y: Variables and control structures allow us to create programs that are dynamic and responsive.
Adding user input makes our programs interactive and useful for real people.
These programming concepts are the building blocks of all software applications.
eg: We create a program that uses variables to keep score in a game.
We use IF-ELSE statements to make our program respond differently to different inputs.
We implement loops that repeat actions until a condition is met.
We add user input so people can interact with our program through clicks or typing.
We debug our program by testing different scenarios and fixing errors.
We combine multiple control structures to create a more complex, useful program.
